title: Fix worktree artifact lookup key mismatch with task IDs
analysis:
Bug: discover_orchestrators() in orchestrator.py keys the worktrees dict by raw subdir.name (e.g. 'task-7'). The template tasks.html does orch.worktrees.get(tid, {}) where tid = task.id|string — always a plain numeric string like '7'. When the worktree directory is named 'task-7' (the convention used by git_ops.py create_worktree), the lookup fails silently and every task shows dashes for Phase, Iterations, Reviews, and Modules columns.

On main (after revert 8b09a69), line 219 reads: worktrees[subdir.name] = read_task_artifacts(subdir). No _extract_task_id helper exists.

Fix (option a from task description): Add _extract_task_id(dirname) helper that normalizes directory names to numeric task ID strings ('task-7' → '7', '7' → '7', invalid → None), then wire it into discover_orchestrators(). The template needs no changes.

Prior attempt history: A previous implementation was merged to main (b986c46) but reverted (8b09a69) due to post-merge verification failure. That attempt had a duplicate _extract_task_id definition (one at file top, one near discover_orchestrators). This plan places the helper in exactly ONE location, directly above discover_orchestrators, and includes no extraneous changes.

This worktree has prior commits re-implementing the fix plus an uncommitted change removing the duplicate. The implementation agent should verify correctness — the core logic is sound but must be clean: one function definition, no duplicates.

Test runner: cd dashboard && uv run python -m pytest tests/test_orchestrator.py -x --tb=short -q
All 33 existing tests pass in the current working tree state.

prerequisites.description: Verify worktree state is clean and tests pass. The worktree has prior commits from a reverted attempt. If there are uncommitted changes (duplicate _extract_task_id removal), stage and commit them first. Ensure no duplicate function definitions exist in orchestrator.py.

steps.description: Write failing unit tests for _extract_task_id helper in a new TestExtractTaskId class in test_orchestrator.py. Test cases: (1) 'task-7' → '7', (2) '33' → '33', (3) 'task-123' → '123', (4) 'task-' → None, (5) '' → None, (6) 'random-dir' → None, (7) 'task-abc' → None. Tests should fail with ImportError if function doesn't exist, or pass if already implemented from prior commits.

Implement _extract_task_id(dirname: str) -> str | None in orchestrator.py, placed directly above discover_orchestrators (not at the file top — avoid the duplicate issue from the prior attempt). Logic: if dirname starts with 'task-', strip prefix and return remainder only if non-empty and all digits; if dirname is itself a non-empty digit string, return as-is; otherwise return None. Verify exactly ONE definition exists in the file. All step-1 tests pass.

Write two failing integration tests in TestDiscoverOrchestrators: (1) test_worktree_keyed_by_task_id_not_dirname — creates worktree dir 'task-7' with .task/ artifacts, asserts '7' in worktrees and 'task-7' not in worktrees; (2) test_non_task_worktree_dirs_excluded — creates 'task-3', '5', and 'tmp-backup' under .worktrees/, asserts '3' and '5' in worktrees, 'tmp-backup' and 'task-3' not in worktrees. Tests fail if discover_orchestrators still uses raw subdir.name.

Wire _extract_task_id into discover_orchestrators() worktree scan loop. Replace `worktrees[subdir.name] = read_task_artifacts(subdir)` with: `task_id = _extract_task_id(subdir.name); if task_id is not None: worktrees[task_id] = read_task_artifacts(subdir)`. All tests pass including existing test_combines_process_and_worktree_data (regression guard). Run full test suite: cd dashboard && uv run python -m pytest tests/ -x --tb=short -q

design_decisions.decision: Normalize directory names in Python (option a) rather than changing the Jinja template lookup
design_decisions.rationale: The template already correctly uses task.id|string which produces the natural numeric lookup key. Fixing at the data layer is testable without template rendering, keeps templates simple, and avoids coupling the template to the orchestrator's directory naming convention.

design_decisions.decision: Place _extract_task_id as a single definition directly above discover_orchestrators
design_decisions.rationale: The prior attempt (reverted) had duplicate definitions causing verification failure. One copy, near the only caller, is clean and maintainable. Underscore prefix marks it module-private.

design_decisions.decision: Require digit-only task IDs after prefix stripping; return None for non-matching dirs
design_decisions.rationale: All Taskmaster task IDs are numeric. Requiring digits prevents accidental inclusion of non-task directories like 'task-logs' or 'tmp-backup'. Returning None lets the caller skip cleanly with a simple if-guard.

design_decisions.decision: Support both 'task-{id}' and plain '{id}' directory naming conventions
design_decisions.rationale: git_ops.py create_worktree uses branch_name as the directory name. The orchestrator passes task-specific names. But plain numeric directories may exist from tests, manual worktrees, or the dark-factory orchestrator itself (which uses numeric dir names like .worktrees/33/). Supporting both prevents regressions.

reuse.how: Follow the same pattern: DashboardConfig(project_root=tmp_path), mock find_running_orchestrators, create dirs under tmp_path/.worktrees/. Reuse for new integration tests.
Returns project_root / '.worktrees'. Tests create directories there; discover_orchestrators() scans it automatically. No config changes needed.
No modifications needed. It operates on Path objects and is called identically; only the dict-keying in discover_orchestrators changes.
This test uses a plain-numeric directory '7' and asserts key '7' in worktrees. Passes unchanged after the fix, confirming backward compatibility with numeric-only dir names.
